role: First Deputy Speaker, 11th Majlis (2020–2024); 2021 presidential candidate
bio: Amir-Hossein Ghazizadeh Hashemi is a hardline politician from Mashhad who served as First Deputy Speaker of the 11th [Majlis](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) under Ghalibaf (2020–2024). He was one of only seven candidates approved by the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) from 585 applicants in the 2021 presidential election, finishing fourth. A physician by training, he represents the [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps)-aligned principlist networks that swept the 11th Majlis.
editorial_notes: Born 1971 in Mashhad. Ran in 2021 presidential election; finished 4th. One of 7 Guardian Council-approved candidates from 585 applicants.
